items.title: dbAdmin
items.text:
This MODX Extra adds a custom manager page that allows you to administrate your databases in MODX.

The functionality is quite limited at the moment, but the features will make the developers' lives a little easier.

items.title: Requirements
items.text:
• MODX Revolution 2.8+
  • PHP 7.4+

items.title: Features
items.text:
• View tables
  • Rename, truncate and delete tables
  • Export tables to an SQL file
  • Search for a table by name or class
  • Execute SQL queries

items.title: License
items.text: The project is licensed under the GPLv2 license.

items.title: Translations
items.text: Translations of the package can be made for the Default Lexicon and the Permissions Lexicon

items.title: Changelog
items.text:
All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

The format is based on Keep a Changelog, and this project adheres to Semantic Versioning.

items.title: Fixed
items.text: • Fix installation error

items.title: Added
items.text:
• Refresh all table classes by button
  • Table classes are prepared for MODX 3.x too

items.title: Changed
items.text: • Use ANALYZE TABLE to get the right sizes in InnoDB tables

items.title: Changed
items.text: • Improved permission resolver

items.title: Changed
items.text: • Completely refactored code.

items.title: Added
items.text:
• Automatically assign package classes to database tables.
  • Edit button for each database table.

items.title: Added
items.text:
• Compatibility with MODX 3.
  • Use .core_path setting to get the table class name.

items.title: Added
items.text:
• Added the horizontal scrolling for a table window.
  • Added output type - var_export or print_r.
  • Added syntax highlighting for sql.
  • Now, instead of the "query error", a full description of the error is displayed.

items.title: Added
items.text: • Changed file name format of database backup to "database_date_time.sql". For example, "modx_20160317_090155.sql".

items.title: Added
items.text: • Add support of PHP 5.3.

items.title: Fixed
items.text: • Fixed some bugs.

items.title: Added
items.text:
• Added the ability to edit the selected cell in tables with a class or remove entire row.
  • Auto-update the list of tables.
  • Added Magic button to define a class for table.
  • Added 'Select from' button to the tables grid that adds 'Select from' script for selected table to the SQL query editor.

items.title: Added
items.text:
• Added autocreation a folder for export operation.
  • Added the table access_namespace for MODX 2.4.0.

items.title: Fixed
items.text: • Fixed the error of checking update (wrong package name).

items.title: Added
items.text:
• Auto synchronization instead of the button.
  • Added a "need update" marker (by experiment).

items.title: Fixed
items.text: • Fixed the error of renaming table.

items.title: Added
items.text: • Initial release

items.title: Contributors
items.text:
The dbAdmin project was started in 2015 by Sergei Shlokov and was updated for MODX 3.x in 2023 by Thomas Jakobi.

Many thanks to all who contributed, whether by creating pull requests, submitting bug reports, or donating.

items.title: Install from MODX Extras
items.text: Search for dbAdmin in the Package Manager of your MODX installation and install it in there.

items.title: Manual installation
items.text:
If you can't access the MODX Extras Repository in your MODX installation, you can manually install dbAdmin.

  • Download the transport package from MODX Extras (or one of the pre built transport packages in _packages)
  • Upload the zip file to your MODX installation's core/packages folder or upload it manually in the MODX Package Manager.
  • In the MODX Manager, navigate to the Package Manager page, and select 'Search locally for packages' from the dropdown button.
  • dbAdmin should now show up in the list of available packages. Click the corresponding 'Install' button and follow the instructions to complete the installation.

items.title: Build it from source
items.text: To build and install the package from source you could use Git Package Management. The GitHub repository of dbAdmin contains a config.json to build that package locally. Use this option, if you want to debug dbAdmin and/or contribute bugfixes and enhancements.

items.title: Usage
items.text: The custom manager page contains two tabs.

items.title: Tables
items.text:
The database tables grid in the Tables tab shows the tables of the current database. Each table can be linked with the MODX system and extras object classes. The content of each table that has the right object class assigned can be edited and rows of the table can be deleted. The object classes are automatically assigned on base of the existing namespaces in MODX during the installation of dbAdmin.

The custom manager page is quite simple and intuitive. If you need to download the entire database, press the Export Database button. If you like to export, truncate or delete multiple tables, you can select the tables with the checkbox and use the Bulk Actions button. To work with one table, you can use the icons in the Actions column.

items.title: SQL Queries
items.text:
Another feature of the custom manager page is to execute SQL queries in the SQL tab. In the input field at the top you can insert standard MySQL queries and execute them with the Execute button. All tables that are linked with an object class can be addressed with the following syntax:

select pagetitle, uri from {modResource}

The query result is shown at the bottom with the print_r or var_export syntax.

items.title: Permissions
items.text:
To work with the component the user must have appropriate rights:

  • tables_list - Permission to display the tables grid.
  • table_view - Permission to view the table content.
  • table_save - Permission to save the table data.
  • table_truncate - Permission to delete all records from a table (truncate table).
  • table_remove - Permission to delete a table (drop table).
  • table_export - Permission to export a table.
  • sql_query_execute - Permission to export a table.

A sudo user is allowed to do everything.
